Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (Shi Chinping or Si Chin Ping) is the President of the People’s Republic of China, General Secretary of the Communist Party, and head of the State Central Military Commission. He was born on June 15, 1953 in Fujian province. His father was Shi Chungshun, who was a former leader of the Communist Party of China. Xi Jinping began his political career in his birthplace Fujian province and later served as the party chief of Zhejiang province. He entered central-level politics by taking responsibility as the party chief of Shanghai.
Xi Jinping is famous for a strong stance against corruption and liberal policies regarding political and market economic reforms. He was nominated as General Secretary of the Communist Party of China due to current responsibilities and special policies. His political influence and policies have established him as the main leader of the Communist Party of China. He also plays an important role in the military field as Chairman of the Central Military Commission of the Communist Party of China.
Xi Jinping’s biography has gained a place as an important part of China’s political and social development. His policies express his involvement in the establishment of the Communist Party of China and state policies.
